title: Housing Subsidies & Stability
concept_highlight: A classic RCT — OLS with controls recovers the average treatment effect directly.
query: Does providing housing subsidies improve the stability of housing situations?
dataset_description: Randomized Control Trial from the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) studying the impact of a housing subsidy on recipients' housing stability. Variables: recipient_age, monthly_income, is_homeowner, has_dependents, lives_in_rural_area, received_subsidy (binary treatment), housing_stability (outcome).

title: Online Learning & Test Scores
concept_highlight: Panel data with staggered adoption — TWFE DiD estimates the policy effect while controlling for school and year fixed effects.
query: Can the introduction of online learning platforms improve students' test scores in high schools?
dataset_description: 10-year panel study from the Department of Education evaluating the impact of e-learning platform implementation on average test scores. Schools adopted the platform at different times (staggered treatment). Variables: school_id, academic_year, e_learning_implemented (binary treatment), average_test_score (outcome), average_class_size, school_funding, urban_location, high_school_level.

title: Public Smoking Bans & Lung Cancer
concept_highlight: The canonical DiD teaching example — when states adopt smoking bans at different times, do lung cancer rates fall?
query: Can implementing public smoking bans result in a decrease in lung cancer rates in the United States?
dataset_description: State-year panel (100 states, 10 years) tracking whether each state had a public smoking ban in place, lung cancer rates, and state-level covariates. Variables: state, year, smoking_ban (binary treatment), lung_cancer_rate (outcome), gdp_per_capita, population_density, urban_area, healthcare_spending.

title: New Drug & Recovery Rate
concept_highlight: Patients aren't randomly assigned to the drug — but doctor experience is (as-if) random and only affects recovery through whether it's prescribed. A textbook instrumental variable.
query: Will the administration of a new drug result in improved patient recovery rates?
dataset_description: Large-scale hospital study examining the impact of a new drug on patient recovery. Treatment is endogenous (sicker patients may be more/less likely to get the drug), so doctor experience is used as an instrument — it affects whether the drug is prescribed but has no direct effect on recovery conditional on treatment. Variables: patient_age, bmi, cholesterol_level, blood_pressure, pre_existing_condition, smoker_status, drug_administration (treatment), recovery_rate (outcome), doctor_experience (instrument).

title: Tutoring Hours & Academic Performance
concept_highlight: Students self-select into tutoring based on unobserved ability — tutor availability (exogenous) is used as an instrument for hours of tutoring received.
query: Does participating in more hours of tutoring cause improved academic performance among students?
dataset_description: National education study examining the impact of tutoring on academic performance. Because more-motivated students may seek more tutoring, tutor_availability is used as an instrument — it varies across schools but has no direct effect on performance conditional on hours received. Variables: student_age, parent_education, school_quality, gender, minority_status, low_income_status, tutoring_hours (treatment), academic_performance (outcome), tutor_availability (instrument).

title: Weekend Coaching Program (RDD)
concept_highlight: Students below a pre-program GPA cutoff of 13 were given priority access — comparing students just above and just below the cutoff estimates the local treatment effect.
query: Does participating in the weekend coaching program improve the academic performance of high school students?
dataset_description: State Department of Education evaluation of a weekend coaching program targeted at low-performing students. Students with pre-program GPA below 13 were given priority access (sharp discontinuity). Variables: student_age, parent_education_level, low_income_status, gender, pre_program_gpa (running variable), program_participation (treatment; 1 = below cutoff), post_program_gpa (outcome).

title: Social Media Campaigns & Elections
concept_highlight: Candidates choose whether to run social media campaigns — propensity score matching balances observed covariates between the two groups to estimate the treatment effect.
query: Does the use of social media campaigns increase the chances of winning local elections in the United States?
dataset_description: Observational data on local election candidates examining whether running a social media campaign increases the probability of winning. Matching is used to balance observed covariates between candidates who ran social media campaigns and those who didn't. Variables: candidate_age, experience_years, campaign_spending, female_candidate, minority_candidate, incumbent_candidate, social_media_campaign (treatment), election_won (outcome).

title: CBT & Stress Reduction
concept_highlight: Unobserved patient factors confound CBT and post-treatment stress — but the mid-treatment stress score is a measured mediator, enabling the front-door criterion.
query: Can attending a series of cognitive-behavioral therapy sessions help reduce stress levels?
dataset_description: Mental health facility study on the impact of CBT on stress levels in patients with anxiety or depression. Unobserved severity factors confound the CBT-outcome relationship, but mid_treatment_stress_score is a measured mediator on the pathway CBT → mid-stress → post-stress, satisfying the front-door criterion. Variables: participant_age, counselling_sessions, anxiety_diagnosis, depression_diagnosis, antidepressant_use, cbt_intervention (treatment), mid_treatment_stress_score (mediator), post_treatment_stress_score (outcome).
